script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
Your focus isn't broken. Your rhythm is.

[PROBLEM 2-6s]
You can't concentrate. You reach for coffee.
You try another productivity app.

[REFRAME 6-16s]
Focus follows energy. Energy follows rhythm.
When you align your tasks with your biological peaks,
focus happens naturally.

[TIP 16-24s]
Peak focus: first 3-4 hours after waking.
Low focus: 7-9 hours after waking.
Schedule accordingly.

[CTA 24-30s]
VOLT Sleep maps your rhythm. Free. Link in bio.
caption: ** Focus isn't a skill problem. It's a timing problem. When are your peak hours?

script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
Peak performance isn't about discipline. It's about timing.

[PROBLEM 2-6s]
You grind through the afternoon.
Force yourself to work when your body says stop.

[REFRAME 6-16s]
Elite athletes don't train the same intensity all day.
They peak, rest, peak again.
Your brain works the same way.

[TIP 16-22s]
Work in 90-minute blocks. Take real breaks.
Match your hardest tasks to your highest energy.

[CTA 22-28s]
Link in bio — free energy profile in 30 seconds.
caption: ** Athletes don't grind 8 hours straight. Why do we expect our brains to? What's your peak time?

script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
There's a reason your coffee stops working at 3 PM.

[PROBLEM 2-6s]
The coffee hits. You feel alert for 20 minutes.
Then you're back to dead.

[REFRAME 6-16s]
By 3 PM, your adenosine buildup is winning.
Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, but they're overwhelmed.
Adding more caffeine just delays the crash — and steals your sleep.

[TIP 16-22s]
Replace the 3 PM coffee with 10 minutes of movement.
Walk. Stretch. The effect lasts longer.

[CTA 22-28s]
I built an app that gives you the right action at the right time. Free.
caption: ** Coffee stops working because your brain chemistry fights back. What's your 3 PM go-to?
